topic: Aspects of Political Life in the German-speaking World
subject: german

items.q: How many federal states ('Bundesländer') does Germany have?
items.why: Students underestimate; Germany has 16 Bundesländer, each with its own parliament, a key feature of its federal system.

items.correct: Germany's Basic Law, its constitution since 1949
items.why: Students think it is a minor statute; the Grundgesetz is the founding constitution of the Federal Republic.

items.q: What is the 'Fünf-Prozent-Hürde' in German elections?
items.correct: A party must win at least 5% of votes to enter parliament
items.why: Students misread it; the 5% threshold keeps tiny parties out of the Bundestag, shaping the party system.

items.q: Where does the verb go after 'weil'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: end of the clause. “second position” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: 16. “10” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was the Grundgesetz adopted?
items.why: Exact answer: 1949. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
